currentFocus: Make current documentation easy to navigate and authoritative while preserving historical RE, release, Lore, and campaign evidence as clearly derived or historical material.

currentCounters.catalogRows: 29/29 target specimen identity and policy metadata rows

currentDocsTruth.release: v1.0.9 is the latest published unsigned portable WinUI ZIP; historical v1.0.7 and v1.0.8 material remains historical.
currentDocsTruth.product: WinUI 3 is the only forward-facing GUI lane. Electron, WPF, and the old Python GUI are archived references.
currentDocsTruth.re: reverse-engineering/ is canonical. lore-book/reverse-engineering/ is a tracked derived mirror pending a safer generated projection.
currentDocsTruth.lore: The release contains 949 offline documents selected from Lore, roadmap, and technical RE material. The count proves packaged breadth, not narrative completeness, editorial approval, freshness, provenance/rights review, or broad public-safety approval.
currentDocsTruth.rebuild: No complete rebuild runtime, renderer, gameplay loop, or parity proof exists. Current maintainers have read GPL source and detailed RE material, so near-term implementation must be labeled RE-informed unless a separately staffed sealed-spec process supports a clean-room claim.
currentDocsTruth.authorityMap: roadmap/repo-structure-and-archive-map.md now classifies current front doors, canonical sources, protected projections, history, and local payloads with explicit conflict rules.
currentDocsTruth.contributorPath: npm test is the verified common active-product baseline; normal setup no longer requires submodules or archived Electron npm installation.
currentDocsTruth.saveOptionsSafety: Save Lab calculates an app-owned patched-output default without creating it. Guarded writers resolve DOS drive aliases to physical local paths, hold physical ancestor identities, and reject in-place, aliased, reparse-routed, reserved-device, device/network, wrong-type, and installed-game-tree destinations. Safe-copy control edits require a minted capability for the canonical app-owned GameProfiles root.

activeBlockers: CURRENT_CAPABILITIES.md and reverse-engineering/RE-INDEX.md bury orientation beneath evidence streams.
release/readiness/ and tools/ lack useful generated front-door indexes for their scale.
The reader-facing Lore pack needs an explicit editorial allowlist, third-party-text inventory, rights/provenance review, and generated canonical projections before another release.
The grouped WinUI action/reaction map omits four Save Lab handler families and claims twelve matrix fields while the TSV currently has nine.
External Cursor/Grok review is degraded by sandbox/authentication constraints; focused Codex review is being used for the current safe slice.
nextSteps: Create generated front-door indexes before moving or deleting large tools, release, or RE evidence trees.
Add a Lore manifest/allowlist and rights/provenance inventory before claiming completeness or publishing another Lore pack.
